channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BJP Confirms Seat-Sharing with Regional Allies Ahead of Assam Assembly Polls
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has confirmed its seat-sharing arrangement with two of its three regional allies ahead of the upcoming Assam Assembly elections, Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma ann...

item.title: Canadian lawyers debate banning NDAs in sexual harassment cases amid ethical concerns
item.description: Ashley Chand, a former director of administration at Vancouver’s Murphy Battista law firm, alleges sexual harassment by a senior lawyer at the firm’s 2023 Christmas party. Chand reported the incident ...

item.title: Mtwara District Commissioner calls for traders’ cooperation in TRA tax education campaign
item.description: The Mtwara District Commissioner, Abdallah Mwaipaya, has made a public appeal for traders to fully cooperate with the Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) during their ongoing tax education campaign. On M...

item.title: Amboseli Main Airstrip Temporarily Closed After Heavy Rains
item.description: The Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) has temporarily stopped flights at the main Amboseli airstrip after heavy rains flooded the area, making it unsafe for planes. The closure affects airlines, tour opera...

item.title: PAGASA Confirms La Niña Has Ended
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has officially announced the conclusion of the La Niña weather phenomenon in the country. La Niña, which is ch...

item.title: SAPS tender under investigation as Suleiman Carrim testifies about dealings with Vusimuzi Matlala
item.description: North West businessman Suleiman Carrim testified at the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry regarding his financial involvement with alleged crime figure Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala. The hearing began with tens...

item.title: Mother campaigns for tougher sentences after driver who killed her teenage daughter on zebra crossing is released early
item.description: Sian Morgan, the mother of 15-year-old Keely Morgan, has expressed profound disbelief and anger after the driver responsible for her daughter's death was released from prison after serving only six-an...

item.title: Highlights from Canberra's 100th Canberra Cup and Black Opal Stakes Day
item.description: Canberra celebrated a landmark weekend at Thoroughbred Park with the 100th Canberra Cup coinciding with Black Opal Stakes Day. The event drew a young, lively crowd of 9,727, marking a 15 per cent incr...

item.title: Tanzania Showcases Sustainable Tourism and Cultural Heritage at ITB Berlin 2026
item.description: Tanzania has made strong presence at ITB Berlin 2026, showing itself as one of Africa’s most lively and diverse travel destinations. The Tanzanian delegation, led by the Tanzania Tourist Board (TTB) a...

item.title: Senator Banigo Stresses Need for Girl-Child Mentoring and Inclusive Leadership at AWLO Event
item.description: Senator Ipalibo Harry Banigo, wey dey represent Rivers West for Senate, don yarn say Nigerians, especially women, suppose sabi say leadership dey everywhere for society and everybody na leader for im ...

item.title: Pakistan Navy starts Operation Muhafiz-ul-Bahr to secure vital sea trade routes
item.description: The Pakistan Navy has initiated Operation Muhafiz-ul-Bahr with the aim of protecting the country's maritime trade and national shipping lanes. According to the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), ...

item.title: Reflecting on 50 Years of Blue Jays Baseball: Share Your Earliest Memories
item.description: As the Toronto Blue Jays mark 50 seasons of baseball, CTV News is inviting Canadians to share their earliest memories of the team. The article highlights the significance of the Blue Jays in Canadian ...

item.title: Marcoleta questions Lacson on Blue Ribbon Committee handling of ex-Marines' cash claims
item.description: Senator Rodante Marcoleta raised concerns in the Senate regarding the handling of allegations by 18 alleged former Marines, who claimed they delivered suitcases of cash from former Congressman Zaldy C...

item.title: Springbok Sevens claim back-to-back HSBC SVNS title with 38-12 win over Spain in Vancouver
item.description: The Springbok Sevens, popularly known as the Blitzboks, secured their third HSBC SVNS tournament victory of the 2026 season by dominating Spain 38-12 in the final at BC Place in Vancouver on Sunday (M...

item.title: How to Plan and Build a Decent Home for Your Parents in Kenya
item.description: This article talks about the growing desire among many Kenyan adults to honour their parents by building them a decent, comfortable home after years of sacrifice and struggle. It explains that for man...

item.title: Iran Appoints Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader Amid Rising Middle East Tensions and Oil Price Spike
item.description: The Middle East is witnessing heightened tensions after Iran appointed Mojtaba Khamenei as its new supreme leader following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on March 1. This transition has intensif...

item.title: Major Decline in Indian Stock Markets: Key Factors Behind Sensex and Nifty Fall
item.description: On a turbulent trading day, Indian stock markets witnessed significant losses, with Sensex dropping by approximately 1,300 points and Nifty falling by 420 points. Analysts attribute this sharp decline...

item.title: Gun violence should be treated as a public health crisis in South Africa
item.description: Every day in South Africa, around 30 people are shot dead and another 43 survive gunshot wounds, equating to more than one shooting every 20 minutes. The article highlights the broader impact beyond d...

item.title: AI Restores Bobby Rush's Voice for Jesse Jackson Jr. Endorsement Ad
item.description: Former longtime Chicago Congressman Bobby Rush, whose vocal cords were damaged by throat cancer, appears in a new campaign ad endorsing Jesse Jackson Jr.'s bid to reclaim the 2nd Congressional Distric...

item.title: The History of Nuclear Weapons in Africa and the Continent’s Nuclear-Free Commitment
item.description: Africa was once home to nuclear weapons, albeit briefly, with South Africa under the apartheid regime being the only country on the continent to have developed six air-deliverable nuclear bombs betwee...
